title: El Mayo Sentencing Five Days Out — All Pre-Hearing Filings Submitted to Brooklyn Court
detail: With the prosecution's April 6 rebuttal deadline passed, all pre-sentencing documents are now on the record in the case of Ismael 'El Mayo' Zambada García ahead of his April 13, 2026 sentencing hearing in Brooklyn federal court. El Mayo, 77, faces mandatory life imprisonment plus a $15 billion forfeiture judgment after pleading guilty in August 2025 to leading a continuing criminal enterprise (RICO) and conspiracy counts covering decades of drug trafficking. Defense attorneys are expected to argue the circumstances of El Mayo's alleged coerced transfer to Texas by Joaquín Guzmán López in mitigation. The sentencing arrives as legal pressure on Mexico's cartel ecosystem intensifies: CJNG co-founder Erick Valencia-Salazar 'El 85' pleaded guilty on April 7, and CJNG successor O3 leads a fractured organization under Operativo Enjambre pressure. Analysts note that both Sinaloa and CJNG now have their founding leadership either dead, imprisoned, or in US legal jeopardy — an unprecedented convergence in a single 45-day window.
